reason: The text features Moses and Aaron as named individual characters with implied professions (leaders/prophets). They are addressed directly by God with specific commands and instructions, constituting dialogue involving them. These are concrete individual characters, not abstract collective groups, making them 'regular people' in the narrative sense. The passage shows God calling to them (וַיִּקְרָא֩ לְמֹשֶׁ֨ה וּֽלְאַהֲרֹ֜ן) and delivering directives to them, establishing their participatory role in the dialogue.
